section.heading: Office of the Chief Scientist

content.p: The Secretary shall establish within the Office of the Commissioner an office to be known as the Office of the Chief Scientist. The Secretary shall appoint a Chief Scientist to lead such Office.

subsection.heading: Duties of the Office
subsection.chapeau: The Office of the Chief Scientist shall—

paragraph.content: oversee, coordinate, and ensure quality and regulatory focus of the intramural research programs of the Food and Drug Administration;

track and, to the extent necessary, coordinate intramural research awards made by each center of the Administration or science-based office within the Office of the Commissioner, and ensure that there is no duplication of research efforts supported by the Reagan-Udall Foundation for the Food and Drug Administration;
develop and advocate for a budget to support intramural research;
develop a peer review process by which intramural research can be evaluated;

paragraph.chapeau: identify and solicit intramural research proposals from across the Food and Drug Administration through an advisory board composed of employees of the Administration that shall include—

subparagraph.content: representatives of each of the centers and the science-based offices within the Office of the Commissioner; and
experts on trial design, epidemiology, demographics, pharmacovigilance, basic science, and public health; and

paragraph.content: develop postmarket safety performance measures that are as measurable and rigorous as the ones already developed for premarket review.

section.sourceCredit: (June 25, 1938, ch. 675, § 1010, formerly § 910, as added Pub. L. 110–85, title VI, § 602, Sept. 27, 2007, 121 Stat. 898; renumbered § 1010, Pub. L. 111–31, div. A, title I, § 101(b)(2), June 22, 2009, 123 Stat. 1784.)
